name: Pollo Con Mole Verde
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the whole chicken in a casserole dish, cover with the stock, and bring to a simmer. Cover the pot and poach the chicken just below the simmering point over very low heat or in a very low oven (275°F) for 1 hour. Remove the chicken from the stock and let it cool enough to carve.
2. Toast the pumpkin seeds together with the peppercorns and cumin seeds in a hot, ungreased skillet about 5 minutes, stirring constantly to prevent scorching. Put into a blender and grind fine.
3. Remove the outer husks from the tomatillos, put them in a pan with cold water to cover, and bring to a simmer. Simmer for 10 minutes, drain, then add them to the blender along with about half of their liquid and blend to a smooth, thick puree.
4. Chop all remaining ingredients and add them to the blender. Add chicken stock if needed to thin the puree. Pour the sauce into the skillet and simmer about 10 minutes to evaporate some of the liquid.
5. Carve the chicken into 4 or more pieces and add to the sauce, coating the pieces well. If possible, let the chicken sit overnight in the sauce so that the seasoning permeates and the heat from the chili diminishes. Reheat gently so that the sauce does not boil. Serve hot or at room temperature.
